summary: First iteration testing of Revisia app. Backend pytest (8/8 passing) covers health, subjects CRUD + upload, chat sessions lifecycle including Claude Haiku 4.5 integration with subject-context grounding (verified 'Zorbeville' appears in reply), auto-titling, cascade delete, and worksheet generation (multiple_choice with 4 options + with/without subject). Frontend smoke-tested via Playwright on desktop viewport: sidebar with no top bar renders, all 3 nav items (Chat/Subjects/Worksheets) present, Fraunces font confirmed on body, routes /chat /subjects /worksheets all reachable, worksheet form has subject select, topic, count slider, difficulty buttons, question style select, extra instructions, Generate button. No MongoDB _id leaks observed in any API response. No console/runtime errors on any page.

minor.issue: logger is referenced in except block but defined AFTER the @api_router functions in server.py - works because logger lookup is at call time, but is fragile. Not breaking.

action_items: Optional: Move `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` and logging.basicConfig above the route handlers in /app/backend/server.py so the except-block logger reference is defined at import time.
Optional: backend_test returns a value in one function — change to plain pytest assertion (warning only).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 484 lines — still manageable but could be split into routers/subjects.py, routers/chat.py, routers/worksheets.py for better organisation.
Anthropic API key is stored verbatim in /app/backend/.env in repo — fine for preview but ensure it is not committed to source control in production.
build_system_message truncates notes to 8000 chars, worksheet prompt truncates to 6000 — good guard against token blowup.
MongoDB _id is correctly projected out using {'_id': 0} on all reads — no leaks.
All write operations are async and use motor; no blocking calls.
Worksheet JSON parsing handles code-fence stripping and regex fallback — robust.
test_credentials: Not applicable — no auth in app
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_-prefixed subjects, sessions, and worksheets via pytest fixtures
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully passing including real Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 calls (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) via emergentintegrations LlmChat. Subject context grounding verified end-to-end (planted 'Zorbeville' fact in notes → assistant retrieved it). Worksheet generator returns valid 4-option MCQs and respects num_questions exactly. The user has limited £5 API budget — keep prompts short and num_questions <=5 for any retests. Frontend smoke covered; for deeper UI tests next iteration, exercise: create subject + paste notes + save + reload + verify persisted, send chat message + assert markdown rendering, generate worksheet + toggle show/hide answers + click both PDF export buttons + capture downloads (use page.expect_download).
rca of the issue: No issues found. All 8 backend tests pass, all 3 frontend routes render correctly, Claude integration works with subject context, worksheet JSON parsing is robust.
